##NAMES=Carraraite
##RRUFFID=R130410
##IDEAL CHEMISTRY=Ca_3_Ge(SO_4_)(CO_3_)(OH)_6_·12H_2_O
##LOCALITY=Cape d'Or, Cumberland Co., Nova Scotia, Canada
##OWNER=Don Doell
##SOURCE=Don Doell
##DESCRIPTION=White to pale-blue hexagonal prismatic crystals, associated with light green apophyllite, light yellowish grey stellerite, and light pinkish rhombohedral chabazite.
##STATUS=The identification of this mineral is confirmed by single-crystal X-ray diffraction and chemical analysis
##URL=https://www.rruff.net/odr/rruff_sample/R130410
##MEASURED CHEMISTRY=Ca_3.18_(Ge_0.95_Si_0.12_Al_0.04_)(SO_4_)(CO_3_)(OH)_6_·12H_2_O
##DIFFRACTION SAMPLE DESCRIPTION=Single crystal, powder profile is calculated
##CELL PARAMETERS=a: 11.039 b: 11.039 c: 10.597 alpha: 90 beta: 90 gamma: 120 volume: 1118.3 crystal system: hexagonal
##FILETYPE=Raman Processed
##RAMAN WAVELENGTH=780
